reasoning: The major budget reconciliation bill ("One, Big, Beautiful Bill Act") was enacted on July 4, 2025. While this legislation made significant changes to the IRA's clean energy tax credits, including adding Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions and altering Placed in Service (PIS) deadlines for Section 45Y, it notably did not make the domestic content requirement mandatory for the base Section 45Y Production Tax Credit (PTC). Instead, it maintained the domestic content requirement as a bonus "adder" and merely aligned the 48E requirements with the existing 45Y structure. There is no evidence of any other federal bill being proposed or passed before the end of 2025 that makes this specific change. Given the recent passage of the reconciliation bill, the window for such a major structural change in 2025 has essentially closed.
